title: CALLED: Find the Work Only You Can Do

hook_package.central_hook: The world is waiting on the one thing only you can do.
section_hooks: You are not stuck — you are unclaimed.
I answered the call the day I stopped auditioning for my own life.
A calling is not found. It is followed, one step at a time.
The door is open — all that is left is your name on the other side.

slides.title: Phase 1 — The Avatar Section
slides.body: Purpose: before I tell you who I am, I tell you who YOU are. First we tell their story, then we tell ours. #StillCalled
slides.suggested_image: Full-bleed dawn horizon over a quiet city; a single lit window; cinematic, hopeful, typographic title lockup in warm gold.

slides.title: The Chorus
slides.body: The world is waiting on the one thing only you can do.
slides.suggested_image: Pure-typography slide, deep indigo field, the single line set large and centered; no imagery, let the words breathe.

slides.title: Their Current State
slides.body: #StillCalled — and still standing in the very same spot. You have done everything right and somehow you are still waiting for your life to begin.
slides.suggested_image: A capable professional at a stoplight at dusk, hands on the wheel, staring at a green light they haven't driven through yet.

slides.title: The Ache
slides.body: "Is my time ever going to come?" You watch louder, lesser people get chosen, and the quiet question won't leave you alone.
slides.suggested_image: Close portrait, half in shadow, of someone watching applause across a room they are not standing in.

slides.title: Not Stuck — Unclaimed
slides.body: You are not stuck — you are unclaimed. Stuck says the road is blocked. Unclaimed says the road is wide open and no one has said your name yet.
slides.suggested_image: A packed suitcase by an open door, morning light spilling in; the person just out of frame, deciding.

slides.title: The Exhaustion of Proving
slides.body: "I am so tired of auditioning for a life that is already mine." Competence became a costume. You perform 'fine' so well that no one asks if you are.
slides.suggested_image: A person removing a stage mask backstage, shoulders finally dropping, exhaustion and relief on the same face.

slides.title: A Riddle
slides.body: Who am I? I am the room's quiet engine. I make everyone else look ready. I am trusted with everything except the spotlight. Who am I? ... You. And that ends today.
slides.suggested_image: A hand-lettered riddle card on a table beside a single unlit candle and a match.

slides.title: The Aspirational Avatar
slides.body: Now imagine waking already chosen — not chasing permission, but doing the work only you can do, and being paid, seen, and thanked for exactly that.
slides.suggested_image: The same person from the pain slides, now on a sunlit path mid-stride, head up, moving with certainty.

slides.title: What Answering Looks Like
slides.body: It sounds like your own voice, unhedged. It looks like a calendar you actually chose. It feels like Monday stops being a sentence you serve.
slides.suggested_image: Split-frame: Monday-as-dread on the left, Monday-as-purpose on the right, same person, different posture.

slides.title: The Whole Picture
slides.body: Problem, aspiration, and the road between them — this is your story, told back to you so accurately that you lean in. Because I have lived it too.
slides.suggested_image: A wide watercolor map with a 'you are here' marker and a distant lighthouse.

slides.title: Your Story, Then Mine
slides.body: You have a story. So do I — and I only get to tell mine because I have already lived yours. So let me show you where the ache in your chest first found its voice.
slides.suggested_image: A storyteller stepping from shadow into a warm spotlight, turning to face the room.

slides.title: Phase 2 — Your Signature Story
slides.body: Purpose: so that you know that I know exactly where you are standing. Not a resume — a journey. #StillCalled
slides.suggested_image: Sepia-to-color transition of an old family photograph coming alive at the edges.

slides.title: The Section Hook
slides.body: I answered the call the day I stopped auditioning for my own life.
slides.suggested_image: A theatre seat, empty, spotlight fading on an unused stage — the day the auditioning stopped.

slides.title: Before the Titles
slides.body: Before the titles, I was a kid who narrated the sky — certain I was here to say something only I could say.
slides.suggested_image: A child on a rooftop at dusk, arms wide, narrating to the clouds.

slides.title: People Who Look Like Me
slides.body: In every hall I walked, I counted the room. "People who look like me" was a refrain I whispered before I learned to say it out loud.
slides.suggested_image: A lone student in a vast lecture hall, scanning rows of faces, notebook untouched.

slides.title: First in the Family
slides.body: I was the first — the first degree, the first office with a door, the first to be 'set for life.' And the first to feel a life set can also be a life stuck.
slides.suggested_image: Hands framing a diploma on a wall beside a family photo; pride and quiet unease.

slides.title: The Golden Handcuffs
slides.body: The career was real, respected, and safe. That was the trap: it was good enough to keep me from the thing I was actually for.
slides.suggested_image: A polished corner office at night, a beautiful cage of glass and city lights.

slides.title: The Whisper
slides.body: #StillCalled — the whisper I kept turning down. Every promotion turned the volume up, not down.
slides.suggested_image: A person turning down a radio dial that keeps glowing brighter no matter how low it goes.

slides.title: The Leap
slides.body: So I traded the title for the calling. I let the old identity go before I had a new one to hold. I told myself the refrain until my feet believed it: I was built for this.
slides.suggested_image: A figure stepping off a lit dock into fog, one foot already gone, the other lifting.

slides.title: The Unedited Version
slides.body: Nobody believes a perfect hero. So here is the unedited version: I doubted, I under-charged, I cried in a parked car. The power of vulnerability is relatability.
slides.suggested_image: A candid, unposed portrait, tears and a small laugh at once, no retouching.

slides.title: Scars Into Map
slides.body: Then the reframe that changed everything: my scars were not damage. They were the map. Every place I got lost is a place I can now guide you through.
slides.suggested_image: Old scars on a hand slowly rendered as topographic map lines and trail markers.

slides.title: I Deserve To Win
slides.body: I stopped asking permission to matter. "I Deserve To Win" — not at anyone's expense, but at last on my own terms.
slides.suggested_image: A runner at a finish line drawn in chalk that they clearly drew themselves.

slides.title: The Road Not Taken
slides.body: "Two roads diverged in a wood, and I— / I took the one less traveled by, / And that has made all the difference." — Robert Frost. The safe road is crowded for a reason.
slides.suggested_image: A forked forest trail, one path worn and busy, one mossy and quiet and lit.

slides.title: The Gripping Why
slides.body: Here is my why, full voice: I build because someone out there is one honest word away from staying — staying in their life, their gift, their calling. That someone is you.
slides.suggested_image: A single porch light left on against a dark street; someone almost walked away, but didn't.

slides.title: Phase 3 — Transformational Teaching
slides.body: Purpose: to hand you the map, one step at a time, so a calling stops feeling like a mystery and starts feeling like a method.
slides.suggested_image: A large blueprint unrolled on a wooden table, weighted at the corners, a compass on top.

slides.title: The Wayfinder Blueprint
slides.body: A calling is not found. It is followed, one step at a time. The Wayfinder Blueprint is 3 Quests across 5 steps. How do you walk an entire calling? The same way you eat an elephant — bite by bite.
slides.suggested_image: An elegant hand-drawn blueprint titled 'The Wayfinder Blueprint' with three quest banners and five numbered waypoints.

slides.title: A Definition Pair
slides.body: Morning vs mourning. You have been spending your mourning — grieving the life you think you missed — in the exact hours meant for your morning. We are going to trade them back.
slides.suggested_image: Two near-identical dawn windows side by side, one grey with rain, one gold with sun.

slides.title: Quest I — The Purpose Quest
slides.body: The first Quest answers the oldest question: what is the work only you can do? Its affirmation is Openness — you cannot receive a calling with clenched hands.
slides.suggested_image: A quest banner reading 'The Purpose Quest' staked at the trailhead of a mountain path.

slides.title: Step 1 — Name the Ache
slides.body: Step One. The ache is not a malfunction — it is a compass. Where it hurts to stay small is exactly where you are meant to grow large. First we name it precisely.
slides.suggested_image: A compass whose needle points not north but toward a glowing figure of the reader.

slides.title: Step 1 — The Question
slides.body: A thought-provoking question: what did you love before the world told you it wasn't practical? Write the first true answer, not the acceptable one.
slides.suggested_image: A journal open to a blank page, a pen resting, morning coffee steaming beside it.

slides.title: Step 1 — A Story in Three Beats
slides.body: A woman told me she 'just organizes things.' Beat one: her team fell apart without her. Beat two: she rebuilt it in a week. Beat three: she still called it 'nothing special.'
slides.suggested_image: A three-panel comic strip of the same calm figure holding chaos together.

slides.title: Step 1 — Core Teaching
slides.body: Your ache always points at a gift you have been giving away for free. Naming the ache is naming the gift. Unnamed, it leaks. Named, it leads.
slides.suggested_image: A leaking cupped hand of light beside a lantern that has caught and contained the same light.

slides.title: Step 1 — Do This
slides.body: Action steps: 1) List three moments you were thanked and shrugged it off. 2) Circle the skill underneath each. 3) The one that repeats is your ache, wearing work clothes.
slides.suggested_image: A checklist card with three hand-drawn boxes, the third circled in gold.

slides.title: Step 1 — Affirmation: Openness
slides.body: Say it: 'I open my hands. What I am for is allowed to be bigger than what I have settled for.' Openness is the affirmation of the Purpose Quest.
slides.suggested_image: Two open palms lifted into a shaft of light, a small seed resting in them.

slides.title: Step 2 — Read the Map You Already Carry
slides.body: Step Two. You are not missing a map. You are ignoring one. Your history, your wounds, your unreasonable joys — together they are the most detailed map you will ever own.
slides.suggested_image: A worn map tattooed faintly across an open hand, trails matching old scars.

slides.title: Step 2 — A Media Moment
slides.body: Play the ninety-second clip here: a child learning to whistle — the frustration, the accidental first note, the face. That face is what following your map feels like.
slides.suggested_image: A film-strip frame of a delighted child mid-whistle, breath visible in cold air.

slides.title: Step 2 — One-Liner
slides.body: Write this one down: minimizing yourself is putting yourself on sale. Every time you shrink, someone learns your true value from your discount.
slides.suggested_image: A luxury item mislabeled with a scrawled clearance sticker, being peeled off.

slides.title: Step 2 — A Simple Formula
slides.body: The clarity formula: Clarity = Attention x Honesty. Most people have neither the attention to look nor the honesty to admit what they see. You will have both.
slides.suggested_image: A clean chalkboard with 'Clarity = Attention x Honesty' written in confident script.

slides.title: Step 2 — A Life Re-Read
slides.body: One learner mapped her 'wasted' years as a caretaker and found the exact empathy her new practice was built on. Nothing was wasted; it was all reconnaissance for the calling.
slides.suggested_image: A woman tracing a long winding trail on a map and smiling at where it finally leads.

slides.title: Step 2 — Affirmation: Authenticity
slides.body: Say it: 'I read the map I already carry. My real history is my qualification.' Authenticity is the closing affirmation of the Purpose Quest.
slides.suggested_image: A hand pressing a wax seal marked 'true' onto a rolled map.

slides.title: Quest II — The Confidence Quest
slides.body: The second Quest is not about feeling ready — it is about moving while unready. Its affirmation is Revelation: confidence is revealed in motion, never in the mirror.
slides.suggested_image: A quest banner reading 'The Confidence Quest' planted at a foggy ridge crossing.

slides.title: Step 3 — Silence the Auditor
slides.body: Step Three. There is a voice that audits every move before you make it — tallying risks, rehearsing the failure. It is not wisdom. It is fear with a clipboard.
slides.suggested_image: A shadowy figure with a clipboard shrinking as the reader turns on a light.

slides.title: Step 3 — The Question
slides.body: Powerful question: whose voice is the auditor using? Nine times out of ten it is borrowed. Name the lender, and you can start returning the debt.
slides.suggested_image: An audio waveform slowly resolving into a familiar silhouette from someone's past.

slides.title: Step 3 — A Story
slides.body: I once let the auditor talk me out of a talk I was born to give. I sat in the parking lot. Someone else gave a worse version to my exact audience. Never again.
slides.suggested_image: An empty parking space beside a lit venue, a ticket stub on the ground.

slides.title: Step 3 — Core Teaching
slides.body: You do not silence the auditor by winning the argument. You silence it by moving before the argument ends. Action is the only mute button that has ever worked.
slides.suggested_image: A hand pressing a large glowing 'GO' button while alarms fade to grey.

slides.title: Step 3 — Take This With You
slides.body: Downloadable: the Auditor Rebuttal Card — three sentences to say aloud the next time the clipboard comes out. Keep it in your wallet next to your nerve.
slides.suggested_image: A pocket-sized printed card titled 'Auditor Rebuttal' tucked into a worn wallet.

slides.title: Step 3 — Affirmation: Revelation
slides.body: Say it: 'I do not wait to feel ready. I move, and my courage is revealed to me on the way.' Revelation is the opening affirmation of the Confidence Quest.
slides.suggested_image: A path lighting up one stone at a time only as a foot lands on each.

slides.title: Step 4 — Walk Before You're Ready
slides.body: Step Four. Readiness is a story you tell to delay the walk. The path reveals itself to those already walking it — never to those still studying the trailhead.
slides.suggested_image: A hiker three steps onto a path that visibly extends only as far as they've walked.

slides.title: Step 4 — An Anecdote
slides.body: The learners who transform are not the most prepared. They are the ones who booked the room, made the offer, and hit publish while their hands were still shaking.
slides.suggested_image: A trembling hand hovering over a 'publish' key, then pressing it, motion-blurred.

slides.title: Step 4 — Borrowed Authority
slides.body: "Do the thing and you will have the power." — Ralph Waldo Emerson. Power follows the doing. It has never once arrived in advance to reward the hesitant.
slides.suggested_image: An old library portrait of Emerson beside his quote, gilt frame, warm reading lamp.

slides.title: Step 4 — A Life Changed
slides.body: A soft-spoken analyst ran one imperfect workshop for six people. Two of them wept. He later said the walk before he felt ready was the readiest he had ever felt.
slides.suggested_image: Six chairs in a small circle, a nervous facilitator standing, the room leaning in.

slides.title: Step 4 — Do This
slides.body: Action steps: 1) Pick the smallest real version of the scary thing. 2) Give it a date this week. 3) Tell one witness. Motion plus a witness beats motivation every time.
slides.suggested_image: A calendar with this week circled and a single name written in the margin.

slides.title: Step 4 — Affirmation: Confidence
slides.body: Say it: 'I walk before I am ready, and the road rises to meet me.' Confidence is the closing affirmation of the Confidence Quest.
slides.suggested_image: A road subtly tilting upward to meet a walker's next step, dawn ahead.

slides.title: Quest III — The Value Quest
slides.body: The final Quest turns your calling outward. Its affirmation is Love — because value, in the end, is love made useful to someone else.
slides.suggested_image: A quest banner reading 'The Value Quest' at a summit overlooking a valley of lights.

slides.title: Step 5 — Give the Gift Only You Can Give
slides.body: Step Five. A calling is not a possession; it is a delivery. The last step is to hand your gift to the exact people who have been waiting for your particular hands.
slides.suggested_image: A wrapped gift being placed into outstretched hands lit by a single warm lamp.

slides.title: Step 5 — Core Teaching
slides.body: Your gift is not the skill — it is the transformation the skill makes possible. Sell the crossing, not the boat. People do not want the map; they want to arrive.
slides.suggested_image: A ferryman's hand pointing not at the boat but at the far, glowing shore.

slides.title: Step 5 — Visualization
slides.body: Close your eyes. See the one person your work is for. Watch their face the moment they understand they were never broken — only unclaimed. Hold that face. That is the point.
slides.suggested_image: A soft-focus face lighting up with relief and recognition, eyes brimming.

slides.title: Step 5 — Reinforcing Quote
slides.body: "We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give." The Value Quest is where a living and a life finally become the same act.
slides.suggested_image: Two cupped hands passing a small flame between them without either being burned.

slides.title: Step 5 — Affirmation: Love
slides.body: Say it: 'I give the gift only I can give, and in the giving I become who I was called to be.' Love is the crowning affirmation of the Value Quest.
slides.suggested_image: A single candle lighting a dozen others, its own flame undiminished.

slides.title: The Bridge
slides.body: So there is the whole map: name the ache, read your map, silence the auditor, walk unready, give your gift. You already have everything you need. What most people miss next is not information — it is company for the walk. In a moment, I will show you exactly what walking it together looks like.
slides.suggested_image: A trailhead at golden hour where a single path splits into many footprints walking forward side by side.

slides.title: Phase 4 — The Purpose Pitch
slides.body: Purpose: to open a door, not to close a sale. A purpose pitch, never a profit pitch. #StillCalled
slides.suggested_image: A tall lit doorway standing alone in an open field at dusk, path leading to it.

slides.title: The Section Hook
slides.body: The door is open — all that is left is your name on the other side.
slides.suggested_image: A door ajar with warm light spilling out; a name plate that is blank, waiting.

slides.title: Setting the Stage
slides.body: You did not come this far to only come this far. The title of what comes next is not a program name — it is a transformation: from unclaimed to unmistakably called.
slides.suggested_image: A theatre curtain parting on a bright, empty, welcoming stage.

slides.title: Break Free
slides.body: Break free of the loop: prove, exhaust, doubt, repeat. There is a door out of the loop, and it has been unlocked this whole time.
slides.suggested_image: A person stepping out of a spinning revolving door into open air.

slides.title: Introducing The Called Collective
slides.body: So here it is, with zero ambiguity — because people buy clarity. The Called Collective is a 12-week guided journey where you walk the entire Wayfinder Blueprint with a small cohort of the quietly capable, and come out claimed.
slides.suggested_image: A warm circle of chairs around a glowing hearth, a banner reading 'The Called Collective'.

slides.title: The Mechanism
slides.body: The engine is the Wayfinder Blueprint plus weekly Quest sessions, a walking-partner accountability pair, and monthly 'answered' checkpoints. Mindset, action, and a mechanism that makes the walk inevitable.
slides.suggested_image: A clean three-gear diagram labeled Mindset, Action, Accountability turning as one.

slides.title: Benefit — Clarity at Last
slides.body: You will name your calling in plain words you can say without flinching — and know exactly who it is for.
slides.suggested_image: A foggy sign resolving into crisp, readable letters.

slides.title: Benefit — The Auditor, Quiet
slides.body: The clipboard voice loses its authority. You will move before it finishes its sentence.
slides.suggested_image: A clipboard closed and set down on a windowsill, sunlight taking the room.

slides.title: Benefit — A Room That Sees You
slides.body: A cohort of people who recognize your gift on sight — the room you have been the quiet engine of, finally pointed back at you.
slides.suggested_image: A circle of faces turned toward one person, warm and attentive.

slides.title: Benefit — An Offer You Can Make
slides.body: You will craft and deliver the gift only you can give — and practice giving it before the stakes are real.
slides.suggested_image: A gift being rehearsed in a mirror, then handed to a real pair of hands.

slides.title: Benefit — Motion Over Motivation
slides.body: A weekly walk that does not depend on how you feel — a structure that carries you on the days willpower won't.
slides.suggested_image: A moving walkway carrying a calm figure forward past a stalled crowd.

slides.title: Benefit — Proof You Can Feel
slides.body: Monthly 'answered' checkpoints where you see, in writing, how far you have actually come — the receipts your doubt keeps hiding.
slides.suggested_image: A progress journal with checkmarks accumulating down the page.

slides.title: Benefit — A Calling That Pays
slides.body: Value built so relentlessly that price becomes a non-issue — because price only matters in the absence of value.
slides.suggested_image: A scale with 'value' heavy on one side and a small feather labeled 'price' rising.

slides.title: Not Just Self-Help
slides.body: This is not another shelf of self-help you will feel guilty for not finishing. It is a guided crossing with a map, a guide, and company — designed so you actually arrive.
slides.suggested_image: A dusty stack of unread books beside a single open trail map with a lantern.

slides.title: A Proven Framework
slides.body: The Wayfinder Blueprint has walked hundreds of quietly capable people from 'is my time coming?' to 'my time is here.' The path is proven; only your footprints are missing.
slides.suggested_image: Countless faint footprints along a trail, one fresh set beginning at the front.

slides.title: In Their Words
slides.body: "I spent years being indispensable to everyone but myself. Twelve weeks in, I made the offer I had been swallowing for a decade — and someone said yes." — a graduate of the journey, anonymized.
slides.suggested_image: A handwritten testimonial card pinned to a corkboard among many others.

slides.title: Future-Pace
slides.body: Picture ninety days from now: the ache named, the auditor quiet, the offer live. Same you. Different Monday. The only variable is whether you start.
slides.suggested_image: A calendar flipping forward 90 days to a date circled and glowing.

slides.title: For a Deeper Crossing — The Wayfinder VIP Intensive
slides.body: For those who want the fast, private version, The Wayfinder VIP Intensive is a single focused day, one-to-one, where we build your entire calling map and your first real offer in one sitting.
slides.suggested_image: A private study with two chairs, a whiteboard mid-map, and a 'do not disturb' sign.

slides.title: Everything You Receive
slides.body: To be perfectly clear: The Called Collective — 12 weekly Quest sessions, a walking-partner pair, the full Blueprint toolkit, monthly checkpoints; and the option of {OFFER_2} for a private, accelerated crossing.
slides.suggested_image: A neatly laid-out flat-lay of everything included, labeled like a field kit.

slides.title: The Best Version of You
slides.body: This is how you become the best version of you — not a new person, the true one, finally unhidden and put to work.
slides.suggested_image: A double exposure of a hesitant self dissolving into a confident self.

slides.title: Transformation by Choice
slides.body: Transformation is not something that happens to you. It is something you choose, on a specific day, at a specific fork. You are standing at that fork right now.
slides.suggested_image: A single figure at a lit crossroads at dusk, both paths visible.

slides.title: The Founding Cohort
slides.body: Honest scarcity, no fabrication: the first cohort is capped at 20 walking partners so every one is truly seen, and founding members join at the founding rate of $1,500 before it moves to $2,400. When the 20 seats are filled, enrollment closes.
slides.suggested_image: Twenty lanterns on a wall, several already lit, a few still dark.

slides.title: A Community Bonus
slides.body: Founding members also receive lifetime access to the Called Collective alumni circle — the room stays yours long after the 12 weeks end.
slides.suggested_image: A warm alumni gathering years later, the same faces, older and lit up.

slides.title: Lifetime Access
slides.body: Every Quest session is recorded and yours to keep for life. The map does not expire, and neither does your seat at the fire.
slides.suggested_image: A library shelf of labeled session recordings glowing softly.

slides.title: A Real Guarantee
slides.body: And the risk is mine, not yours: walk the first three weeks, do the work, and if the map has not already moved you, ask and receive a full, no-friction, money-back guarantee. You cannot lose what you were built to find.
slides.suggested_image: A handshake over a document titled 'Our Promise', warm and unhurried.

slides.title: Your Two Roads
slides.body: So, two roads: the crowded safe one where the ache stays and next year looks like this one — or the quiet lit one, walked with company, toward the work only you can do.
slides.suggested_image: The forest fork again, now with a lantern lit on the less-traveled path.

slides.title: Your Chance to Break Free
slides.body: This is your chance to break free of the loop for good — not someday, not once things settle, but in the next 12 weeks, on purpose.
slides.suggested_image: A bird lifting off from an open cage door into a bright sky.

slides.title: Answer the Call
slides.body: If your chest tightened even once tonight, that was not nerves — that was recognition. Enroll now in the Called Collective and answer the call while it is still ringing.
slides.suggested_image: A hand reaching for a ringing old telephone glowing on a table.

slides.title: Your Future Is Waiting
slides.body: Your future is not far away. It is standing at the door you have already found, holding it open, wondering what took you so long.
slides.suggested_image: A figure in a lit doorway, hand extended toward the viewer.

slides.title: You Are Worthy of This
slides.body: Hear this plainly: you were always worthy of the calling. Worthiness was never the gate. Willingness is.
slides.suggested_image: A mirror reflecting the reader haloed in soft, deserving light.

slides.title: Join the Movement
slides.body: This is bigger than a program. It is a quiet movement of the capable refusing to stay unclaimed. Join us, and add your footprints to the path.
slides.suggested_image: A long line of diverse people walking a lit trail together toward dawn.

slides.title: This Is Your Moment
slides.body: Not next quarter. Not when the kids are older. This — this ordinary, extraordinary night — is your moment. Moments do not wait; they pass.
slides.suggested_image: A single spotlight finding one face in a crowd, holding on it.

slides.title: Shatter the Ceiling You Built
slides.body: The only ceiling over you is the one you plastered yourself. Reach up. It was always made of paper.
slides.suggested_image: A hand punching easily through a paper 'ceiling' into open sky.

slides.title: Pride, at Last
slides.body: Imagine the pride — not borrowed from a title, but earned from finally doing the work only you can do, for the people only you can reach.
slides.suggested_image: A quiet, private smile of someone reading a thank-you note alone.

slides.title: The Journey Starts Today
slides.body: The journey does not start when you feel ready. It starts the day you decide — and that day can be today.
slides.suggested_image: A sunrise breaking exactly at a trailhead sign reading 'Day One'.

slides.title: Take Your Seat
slides.body: Claim your seat in the founding cohort now, before the twenty lanterns are all lit. Say yes, and let the crossing begin.
slides.suggested_image: A single empty chair in the warm circle with a soft reserved glow.

slides.title: Thank You
slides.body: Whatever you choose tonight: thank you for letting me tell your story back to you, and for trusting me with mine.
slides.suggested_image: A speaker with hand on heart, bowing slightly to a warm, blurred audience.

slides.title: The Chorus, One Last Time
slides.body: The world is waiting on the one thing only you can do.
slides.suggested_image: Return to the pure-typography chorus slide, indigo field, the single line, now with a faint doorway of light behind the words.

slides.title: A Manifesto — Part I
slides.body: You were not made to be manageable. You were made to be met. You were not born to blend — you were born to be the one thing a room did not know it was missing.
slides.suggested_image: Hand-lettered manifesto text over a slow dawn, each line appearing as if spoken.

slides.title: A Manifesto — Part II
slides.body: So rise, quietly capable one. Read your map. Silence the auditor. Walk before you are ready. Give the gift only you can give. The world is waiting — and it has waited long enough.
slides.suggested_image: The dawn now fully broken, the lettering blazing gold, a path leading into light.

slides.title: Finish It in Your Own Words
slides.body: Say it with me, and finish the last line yourself, out loud: I AM CALLED. I AM CAPABLE. I AM ______.
slides.suggested_image: Full-bleed golden field, the affirmation set huge, the final blank a shining underscore for the audience to fill.
